mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discount conditions and lower equity hurdle rates, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when the policy path is easing rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation slowdown in CPIAUCSL reduces expected policy pressure and real-rate risk, supporting higher equity multiples and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-triggered episodes do not beat unconditional 20-day SPX returns by at least 2 percentage points, or if negative returns dominate after inflation slows below 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and lower future demand, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month when unemployment accelerates from already elevated levels.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Broad demand acceleration in RSAFS combined with stronger PAYEMS growth indicates improving nominal spending and payroll income, which lifts earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising retail sales and payroll gains does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in the top demand-expansion quartile.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises faster than GDP or sales proxies, it signals improving industrial supply utilization and cyclical growth breadth, which should lift cyclicals and the index over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change exceeds its 12-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if periods of accelerating industrial production fail to produce above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the effect is negative in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals housing demand weakening before it appears in broader activity, and equity returns over the next month turn negative as growth-sensitive sectors reprice lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-downturn triggers are not followed by below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the trigger occurs without any negative edge relative to unconditional outcomes.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, low UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS gains implies resilient income growth and limited recession pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 6.2% and PAYEMS is rising month over month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment, rising-payroll condition does not generate positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock normalization regime, stable CPIAUCSL with near-zero FEDFUNDS keeps real policy accommodative, supporting risk-taking and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of low policy rates and stable inflation does not outperform either condition separately, or if 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive in the specified recovery regime.
